### Post by own3mall on 2011-06-06
How do you configure DDClient to use the FREEDNS service provided by afraid.org?  

There's a short snippet of info here:

[http://en.gentoo-wiki.com/wiki/Dynamic_DNS](http://en.gentoo-wiki.com/wiki/Dynamic_DNS)

Does this look like the proper config I'd need?

```

# Configuration file for ddclient generated by debconf
#
# /etc/ddclient.conf

protocol=freedns
login=xxx
password='xxx'
webehostin.com,ftp.webehostin.com,mail.webehostin.com,ns5.webehostin.com,ns6.webehostin.com,www.webehostin.com


```Is there anything else I need to change?  Does anyone have a working config for ddclient using freedns.afraid.org?

---

### Post by own3mall on 2011-06-06
Or, should it be more like this:

```

# Configuration file for ddclient generated by debconf
#
# /etc/ddclient.conf

protocol=freedns
use=web, web=checkip.dyndns.com, web-skip='IP Address'
login=xxx
password='xxx'
webehostin.com,ftp.webehostin.com,mail.webehostin.com,ns5.webehostin.com,ns6.webehostin.com,www.webehostin.com

```

---

### Post by papibe on 2011-06-06
I don't see any protocol called freedns. Check [this]("http://sourceforge.net/apps/trac/ddclient/wiki/Protocols").

Regards.

### Post by own3mall on 2011-06-06
> **papibe said:**
> I don't see any protocol called freedns. Check [this]("http://sourceforge.net/apps/trac/ddclient/wiki/Protocols").

Regards.


Right, but it even states:

> 
This is an incomplete list of the services supported by ddclient. If  your favourite dynamic dns provider isn't here, check the result  ddclient --help with the most recent version of ddclient. If it's there,  check the patches section on sf.net and if it's really not supported by  ddclient you can try to modify ddclient yourself. 


Looks like ever since 3.8.0, freedns is supported.  Anyone know how to configure it?

### Post by Don Escobar on 2011-11-20
I'm not sure if you got this working, i did some digging around and found this 

[http://en.gentoo-wiki.com/wiki/Dynamic_DNS]("http://en.gentoo-wiki.com/wiki/Dynamic_DNS")

Scroll all the way to the bottom where they explain that the change to add FreeDNS is recent like you mentioned and give you a config sample. Only way to see it update is to get your ISP to release a new IP to you.

Hope it helps!
